metadata.previous_ramsey_interpretation_after_nar: dominant 1.593 MHz peak plausibly residual detuning from prior center, not 13C claim-grade

analysis_plan.next_if_usable: use corrected-center terminal data to support or reject T2star and 13C candidate status separately
analysis_plan.next_if_nonclaim_grade: if carrier is corrected but SNR/fit remains insufficient, consider higher-shot repeat or det-shift diagnostic rather than a blind repeat

metadata.execute_route_policy: validated auto__ramsey manifest; verified intent plus pre-enqueue advisory before managed execute.
metadata.frequency_reason: narrow weak-pi pODMR after Ramsey gives raw/fit-reference center 3.876116667 GHz; correcting this should move the Ramsey carrier back toward the programmed 2 MHz bin.
metadata.ramsey_grid_reason: det=2 MHz over 0..8 us with 51 points gives Nyquist about 3.125 MHz and bin spacing 125 kHz, covering carrier plus expected ~0.384 MHz 13C sidebands.
metadata.acquisition_reason: 6 averages x 100000 repetitions gives 600000 shots, higher than the first 400000-shot scout while preserving the per-average tracking window; even averages preserve snake-order balance.
comparison_targets: Doherty et al. Physics Reports 528, 1-45 (2013), DOI 10.1016/j.physrep.2013.02.001, for general NV resonance/field context
NV memory Ramsey FFT guardrail: choose det/tau span/points together and keep Nyquist above det plus sideband
metadata.sequence_readout_roles: full_experiment=0 path has readout 1 mS=0 reference before Ramsey and readout 2 post-Ramsey signal.
metadata.intent: Run a corrected-center Ramsey/T2star repeat for image231924_c01 after narrow weak-pi pODMR shifted the usable center upward and downgraded the old 1.593 MHz peak to likely residual detuning.
